description: A side-effecting handler with no idempotency guard, so at-least-once redelivery causes double charges, duplicate inserts, or repeated sends.
body: Failure mode: a function performs a non-idempotent action (charge a card, insert a row, send an email) and the event source redelivers on timeout/error, so the action repeats. Detection signals: duplicate records, double charges, or repeated notifications under load; incidents spike when the source is busy and retries. Do not assume exactly-once delivery. Safer fallback: derive a stable idempotency key, conditionally record it before acting, short-circuit duplicates, and verify with a same-event replay test asserting a single effect.
